source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI. Cover page confirms Lawn Doctor, Inc. 2025 FDD. PDF title metadata confirms 'Lawn Doctor _ 2025 Franchise Disclosure Document'. Holiday Lighting Heroes is a secondary service line offered by some Lawn Doctor franchisees; Item 19 excludes HLH revenues.

brand.parent_company: LD Parent, Inc. / CNL Strategic Capital, LLC

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Comprised of 4 components: (1) $50,000 initial license fee, (2) $70,600 initial training/supply/support fee, (3) $3,350 Turf Tamer Stand-On Applicator Lease deposit (nonrefundable), (4) $3,050 Turf Tamer Power Seeder Lease deposit (nonrefundable). Low end ($123,950) applies where agronomic conditions preclude seeding — Power Seeder removed. Due in full at signing, nonrefundable. VetFran/MinorityFran/GreenFran/First Responder: 10% off the $50,000 license fee portion (not combinable). Additional territory for existing compliant franchisees: $25,000 reduced fee. 5-year employee discount: $10,000 off. Fees received in 2024 ranged from $114,450 to $122,000.

provenance.exact_text: a nonrefundable initial franchise fee of $127,000

royalty_rate.notes: 10% of Net Revenues, payable weekly. 'Net Revenues' = actual gross revenues collected from customers (cash or credit) plus all other revenues, excluding taxes collected and refunds/adjustments. Additional 15% Out-of-Territory Royalty on Net Revenues earned outside Strategic-Partner's Territory (paid on top of 10%).

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Royalty and Service Fee row

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Complex multi-layer marketing structure. Local advertising requirement: must spend the greater of $30,000 or 10% of Net Revenues annually. National Marketing Fund: 2% of Net Revenues (if prior year under $175K) or ~$106/week ($5,500/year) (if prior year over $175K), capped at 5% total with regional. Regional Marketing Funds: up to 5% of Net Revenues in 15 metro areas. New franchisees exempt from national fund for first partial + first full calendar year.

components.name: Local Advertising Requirement
components.notes: Must spend the greater of $30,000 or 10% of Net Revenues annually. Unspent amount due to LDI within 60 days after calendar year end.

components.name: National Marketing Fund
components.notes: 2% of Net Revenues if prior year under $175K; ~$106/week ($5,500/year) if prior year over $175K. New franchisees exempt first partial + first full calendar year. Capped so total (national + regional) does not exceed 5% of Net Revenues.

components.name: Regional Marketing Fund
components.notes: If Business is in a regional fund area (15 regions as of issuance). Adjusted so total (national + regional) does not exceed 5% of Net Revenues.

components.name: Local Marketing Fund
components.notes: Up to 5% of Net Revenues, amount set by majority vote of local franchisees. Credited against local advertising requirement. No local funds currently in existence.

components.name: Centrally Managed Media Boost Program
components.notes: Optional. Only for franchisees who begin after optimal season start and elect supplemental corporate-directed media.

components.name: Holiday Lighting Heroes Advertising
components.notes: Greater of $6,000 or 5% of prior year HLH Net Revenues. Drives media-based leads for holiday lighting services.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Lawn Doctor's marketing structure is unusual in requiring a minimum of the GREATER of $30,000 or 10% of Net Revenues for local advertising alone, on top of national/regional fund contributions. This creates a high marketing floor for low-revenue franchisees. Cannot be expressed as a single rate due to the $30K minimum and tiered national fund structure.

technology_fee.notes: $150/month until cumulative Net Revenues reach $1,000,000, then increases to $250/month. LDI may increase up to $500/month at its discretion. First payment not due until Business commences operations.

provenance.exact_text: Currently starts at $150 with a scheduled increase to $250

transfer_fee.formula: 75% of then-current initial license fee component ($50,000 as of 2025 = $37,500). 10% due at declaration of intent to sell (nonrefundable); remainder due before transfer completion.
transfer_fee.notes: Broker Fee of $25,000 additional if LDI engages a third-party broker to assist in selling the Business.

provenance.exact_text: 75% of the then-current initial license fee component of the initial franchise fee

other_fees.notes: 15% of Net Revenues earned outside Territory, paid IN ADDITION to the 10% Royalty and Service Fee (total 25% on out-of-territory revenue).

provenance.exact_text: 15% of Net Revenues earned outside of Strategic-Partner's Territory

other_fees.notes: First year included in initial franchise fee. May increase, not expected to exceed $1,000/month.
Payable to LDI. Lump sum option: $22,350. Plus sales tax.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Turf Tamer Stand-On Applicator Lease row

other_fees.notes: Payable to LDI. Lump sum option: $18,700. Plus sales tax. Not applicable in areas where agronomic conditions preclude seeding.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Turf Tamer Power Seeder Lease row

other_fees.notes: Up to $2,000 annually, plus actual out-of-pocket attendance costs. Must attend at LDI's request; fee due even if not attending.
Due if LDI engages a third-party broker or consultant on franchisee's behalf to assist in selling the Business.
Highest legal rate for open account business credit, not to exceed 1.5% per month. Current policy: 1% per month. Also: 1% of total amount due per month for late royalty reports; $20 per rejected EFT.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Interest on Late Payments row

other_fees.fee_name: Fine for Failure to Provide Materials for Inspection
other_fees.notes: $500/day plus LDI's reasonable expenses for delay.

other_fees.fee_name: Fine for Use of Unauthorized Advertising

line_items.notes: Includes: Initial License Fee $50,000; Training/Supply/Support $70,600; Stand-On Applicator Lease Deposit $3,350; Power Seeder Lease Deposit $0-$3,050.
Assumes lease from LDI-approved supplier. Covers first month of typical 72-month lease.

line_items.category: Shipping of Turf Tamer Power Seeder
line_items.notes: Cost depends on distance from LDI manufacturing facility. $0 if Power Seeder not applicable.
Lawn care software ~$225/month; Holiday Home Concepts ~$130/month; Serviceminder ~$54-$189/month.
Hardware per LDI specs including QuickBooks Online ($85/month ongoing).

line_items.category: Opening Inventory for lawn care products/materials

line_items.notes: Many franchisees operate from home initially. Approximately 12'x20' space if needed.

line_items.category: Start-Up Equipment Package for holiday lighting

line_items.notes: Covers operating expenses, labor, vehicle lease months 2-3, software fees, rental space, Technology Fees, miscellaneous. Not a break-even estimate.

total_provenance.exact_text: TOTAL ESTIMATED INITIAL INVESTMENT FOR LAWN DOCTOR BUSINESS $150,070 to $177,052

tables.description: 2024 Net Revenues for Strategic-Partners operating 2+ full years, categorized by number of territories operated
tables.population: 205 Strategic-Partners operating Lawn Doctor Businesses for 2+ full years as of December 31, 2024. Of 653 total Businesses operated by 229 Strategic-Partners. Item 19 excludes Holiday Lighting Heroes revenues.
tables.notes: Net Revenues are per Strategic-Partner (all territories combined), not per outlet. 29% at or above average across all categories indicates right-skewed distribution (high performers pulling up the average). LDI compiled from weekly reports; does not verify figures. POSSIBLE TYPO: 4-6 territories lowest shows '$84.503' in PDF — likely $84,503 (period vs comma formatting).

tables.population: 205 Strategic-Partners operating 2+ years with available data as of December 31, 2024
tables.notes: Customer Program Value = total annual value of all programs/services purchased by a customer (active + cancelled, minimum $100 cumulative). Customer Tenure uses creation date with nearest-neighbor imputation for missing dates; excludes pre-1970 and post-2024-01-01. Customer Revenue = payments received in 2024, minimum $100 cumulative. Average customer tenure of 6.15 years is a strong retention signal.

tables.description: 2023 Gross Profit Margin Results as a Percentage of Net Revenues
tables.population: 130 of 205 Strategic-Partners operating 1+ full year as of December 31, 2023. 75 Strategic-Partners excluded due to insufficient information; LDI does not know if inclusion would materially affect results.
tables.notes: Gross Profit Margin = Net Revenues minus Material Costs (treatment products only). Does NOT include royalty (10%) or marketing expenditures (10%+ of Net Revenues). Some franchisees use cash basis, others accrual basis; some follow GAAP, others do not. Effective margin after royalty and marketing is significantly lower. 37% of eligible Strategic-Partners excluded from this table.

tables.description: Historical Net Revenues 2009-2024 for Strategic-Partners operating 2+ full years
tables.population: Strategic-Partners operating 2+ full years as of December 31 of each respective year. Population varies from 155 (2013) to 214 (2009).
tables.notes: 16-year historical trend showing consistent average net revenue growth from $367K (2009) to $1.13M (2024) — a 3.08x increase. Median grew from $207K to $659K (3.18x). System contracted from 214 to 155 Strategic-Partners (2009-2013) before recovering to 205 by 2024. YoY average growth slowed to 1.4% in 2024 (vs 10.3% in 2023). Top performer grew from $2.9M to $11.4M over the period.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 disclosed with 4 tables. Strongest Item 19 disclosure depth in the lawn cohort so far: includes revenue by territory count, customer metrics, gross margin, and 16-year historical trend. All data excludes Holiday Lighting Heroes revenues. Revenue figures are per Strategic-Partner (all territories combined), not per outlet — a multi-territory franchisee's combined revenue is reported as one line. LDI does not verify franchisee-reported weekly revenue figures.

years_reported.notes: All outlets are franchised. No company-owned outlets. Net growth +11.
All outlets are franchised. Net growth +6.

All outlets are franchised. Net growth +23. Terminations dropped to 1 (from 13 in 2022 and 9 in 2023). Transfers also declined to 9 (from 28 in 2022). Projected 18 new outlets in 2025 across 11 states (Table 5).

item_3_litigation.notes: No litigation required to be disclosed in this Item. No bankruptcy either (Item 4).

provenance.exact_text: No litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.
LDI was incorporated in 1967 under the name 'Auto-Lawn of America, Inc.'

warnings: Lawn Doctor is affiliated with Mosquito Hunters (same parent LD Parent, Inc.). Both are in this dataset but in different wedges.

Marketing obligation cannot be expressed as a single rate: local advertising alone requires the greater of $30,000 or 10% of Net Revenues, plus national fund (2% or $5,500/yr), plus regional fund (5%) if applicable. total_ongoing_fee_rate set to null.
Technology fee is tiered by cumulative revenue ($150 below $1M, $250 above, max $500). Recorded as $150 (initial rate).

Item 19 Table A reports per Strategic-Partner (all territories combined), not per outlet. A multi-territory franchisee's combined revenue appears as one entry. This inflates apparent per-brand averages compared to per-outlet reporting.
Item 19 Table C (Gross Profit Margin) excludes 37% of eligible Strategic-Partners due to insufficient data. LDI states it does not know if inclusion would materially affect results.
Item 19 Table A possible typo: 4-6 territories 'Lowest Net Revenues' shows '$84.503' in PDF — likely $84,503. Recorded as 84503.
Transfer fee is formula-based (75% of then-current initial license fee component). As of 2025, this equals $37,500 based on $50,000 license fee.
Out-of-Territory Royalty of 15% is IN ADDITION to the 10% base royalty (total 25% on out-of-territory revenue). This is unusually high and structurally discourages out-of-territory work.
Holiday Lighting Heroes is a secondary service line (14 franchisees as of end 2024). All Item 19 data explicitly excludes HLH revenues.
System has been franchising since 1967 — one of the oldest franchise systems in the U.S. 58-year track record.

null_fields.reason: Complex multi-layer structure: local $30K or 10% minimum + national fund (2% or $5,500) + regional (5%). Cannot express as single rate.
Formula-based: 75% of then-current initial license fee component.
Marketing component is ambiguous (multi-layer with dollar minimums).
